Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Continued To Apr-21-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action For Failure To Serve Summons Within Two Years
Off Calendar Feb-20-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ANTWON CHILDES, SHARON LYLES' Motion To Dismiss Action For Failure To Serve Summons Within Two Years.

The unopposed motion to dismiss this action for delay in prosecution is granted. Plaintiff has not yet served the summons and has not appeared on orders to show cause. "The unexcused failure to serve a summons within the two-year period prima facie constitutes a sufficient ground for dismissal. In such a case there is no requirement for an affirmative showing of prejudice." (Scarzella v. DeMers (1993) 17 Cal.App.4th 1762, 1768-1769 [citation simplified and footnote omitted].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
